title: feat: add test for nested stream and check value has predict_id
body:
This PR is to add test for nested streaming where we have an agent streaming its response and its tools to run some dspy programs.

- I added the test to validate the use case
- I add check the predict_id before attempt to response to client

Related issue: https://github.com/stanfordnlp/dspy/issues/8454

cc @chenmoneygithub, @okhat

step_plan.summary: Make async_streamer in dspy/streaming/streamify.py defensive: check that a ModelResponseStream chunk has a predict_id and that the predict_id maps to registered listeners before indexing into predict_id_to_listener; otherwise yield the chunk unchanged to avoid AttributeError/KeyError in nested streaming. Add/ensure tests cover chunks without predict_id, mapped predict_id dispatch, and unmapped predict_id yielding.

steps.operation: Add defensive predict_id presence and membership check in async_streamer before dispatching ModelResponseStream chunks to listeners
steps.reason: async_streamer currently assumes every ModelResponseStream chunk has a predict_id that indexes into predict_id_to_listener. In nested streaming cases some chunks may lack predict_id or have a predict_id not present in the top-level listener map, leading to AttributeError or KeyError. Adding a check (hasattr(value, 'predict_id') and value.predict_id in predict_id_to_listener) prevents exceptions and preserves backward-compatible behavior by yielding unrelated chunks unchanged.
steps.side_effects: Behavioral: StreamListener instances will only receive chunks that both carry a predict_id and map to a registered listener; chunks without predict_id or with an unmapped predict_id will be yielded unchanged (previously these could cause exceptions). Tests: add/update unit tests for nested streaming (verify no crash when chunks lack predict_id, verify mapped predict_id still dispatched to listeners, and verify unmapped predict_id is yielded). Dependents: code that expected every ModelResponseStream to be intercepted by listeners may see fewer callbacks (only matching predict_id); this change is backward-compatible w.r.t. consumers expecting raw chunk yields. API: no public API changes; only internal dispatching logic is made defensive. Recommend running unit/integration tests for streaming, agent/tool nested streaming scenarios, and any modules that register predict_id-based listeners.
